context: Tests whether the agent follows RSpec structural conventions when writing a service object spec: file pragma, describe format, service spec anchor pattern, external mocking style, and example naming discipline.

checklist.name: Frozen string pragma
checklist.description: The spec file begins with `# frozen_string_literal: true` as its very first line

checklist.name: Full constant path
checklist.description: RSpec.describe references the full constant path `Memberships::UpgradeService` (not a string like 'Memberships::UpgradeService')

checklist.name: describe '.call' block
checklist.description: Contains a `describe '.call'` block wrapping the primary test cases

checklist.name: subject(:result) definition
checklist.description: Defines `subject(:result) { described_class.call(...) }` inside the describe '.call' block

checklist.name: No 'and' in examples
checklist.description: No it/specify description string contains the word 'and' (case-insensitive, as a full word)

checklist.name: No 'should' in examples
checklist.description: No it/specify description string begins with or contains 'should'

checklist.name: External service mocking style
checklist.description: MailgunClient is mocked using `allow(MailgunClient).to receive(...)`, NOT via instance_double

checklist.name: context 'when' naming
checklist.description: Scenario variations use `context 'when ...'` or `context 'with ...'` blocks (not bare describe blocks for scenarios)

checklist.name: Spec file path
checklist.description: The spec is placed at `spec/services/memberships/upgrade_service_spec.rb` (mirrors source path)

checklist.name: No ActiveRecord mocking
checklist.description: Does NOT mock or stub ActiveRecord finders (User.find is not stubbed/mocked)
